user_query:
I’m prepping for our Youth Politics Society meeting and need a local fact-check of several claims. Please write and run a local script or command-line program that: (1) reads input/claims.csv, input/official_stats.csv, and input/accuracy_rules.yaml; (2) applies the rules to compare each claim to the corresponding official statistic; and (3) uses input/context.md to generate meeting notes. Save the outputs to the outputs/ directory as follows:

- outputs/verification_results.json: a JSON array where each object includes at minimum these fields per claim: claim_id, metric, area, claim_value, claim_unit, official_year, official_value, official_unit, difference, tolerance, status (one of "accurate", "inaccurate", "unclear"), and rule_applied (short text), plus a brief note explaining any unclear status.
- outputs/summary_report.md: a concise status summary for the Society, including (a) overall counts of Accurate, Inaccurate, and Unclear (must be consistent with outputs/verification_results.json), and (b) a short narrative summary highlighting the main findings and any patterns by metric or area.
- outputs/meeting_notes.md: meeting notes for the upcoming session, using details from input/context.md. Include sections for Agenda, Findings Summary, and Action Items. Create at least one actionable item for every claim marked Inaccurate or Unclear, referencing the claim_id and what should be followed up. Use the meeting_date from input/context.md, and set each action’s due_date to meeting_date + 7 days in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD). If context provides a default action owner, use it.
- outputs/command.txt: the exact command you used to run your script/program so the outputs can be reproduced.

Verification rules to implement:
- Use year_reference in input/accuracy_rules.yaml to pick the official row for each metric+area.
- If units differ between claim and official, mark status = "unclear".
- For percent units, difference = absolute percentage-point difference; for count units, difference = absolute count difference.
- Compare difference against the metric-specific tolerance in input/accuracy_rules.yaml: status = "accurate" if difference <= tolerance and units match; status = "inaccurate" if difference > tolerance and units match; status = "unclear" if no official match for metric+area+year or units mismatch.

All outputs must be generated by running your local script/command (no manual edits), and the summary and meeting notes must be consistent with the verification JSON. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
